title: Product Offers
description: Commercial offers for a product. Breaking from v1.8: flat offers[] wrapped in offer_groups[] with attribute inheritance (2-level, group then offer). v2.1 (v2.42): scope enum élargi (pre_cart conservé, post_cart ouvert) avec cross-ref funnel.schema.json pour stages funnel post-cart. Lives in brands/{slug}/products/{slug}/offers.json once validated.

_changelog.2.1: v2.42 cleanup: `_schema_version` field root standardisé (format `{name}/{semver}`, remplace `_version` const). Marker DRAFT retiré du title. `meta.scope` passé de const `pre_cart` à enum [`pre_cart`, `post_cart`] pour ouvrir aux funnel stages post-add-to-cart. Cross-ref ajouté vers funnel.schema.json (parent_offer_group). Backward compat strict: lecteurs anciens reçoivent toujours pre_cart par défaut, post_cart est additif. Source canon: PATCH 2A v2.42.
_changelog.2.0: Initial v2.0 (DRAFT pré-cleanup): flat offers[] wrapped in offer_groups[] avec attribute inheritance (2-level). 10 structural types (single|bundle|prepay|subscription|membership|gifting|seasonal|launch|digital|service). C2 membership, C3 intro_phases cascading, C4 early_access cohort_tiers, C5 personalization pointer, C6 commitment structuré, C8 referral product_refs structurés.

_version.description: Backward compat alias of root `_schema_version`. v2.0 instances écrivaient `2.0` ici; v2.1+ readers SHOULD read the root field. Writers MAY echo the semver portion for legacy tooling.

scope.description: v2.1: enum élargi (was const `pre_cart` en v2.0). pre_cart = offers.json describes pre-add-to-cart merchandising (PDP offers, bundle builders, quiz results, subscription choices). post_cart = thank-you upsell, order-confirmation bumps, post-purchase cross-sell. Post-cart funnel stages structure vit dans funnel.schema.json (cross-ref via funnel.parent_offer_group). Binary rule: if it's shown before the customer clicks add-to-cart, scope=pre_cart. Otherwise scope=post_cart.

post_cart.description: v2.1: placeholder pour métadonnées post-cart spécifiques quand scope=post_cart. Stages structurés (upsell, cross_sell, bump, down_sell, re_order_email) vivent dans funnel.schema.json. Ce bloc capture l'intention macro et le pointer funnel. Null tant que scope=pre_cart.

funnel_ref.description: Reference vers funnel_id dans brands/{slug}/products/{slug}/funnel.json (cf funnel.schema.json). Lien upstream offer ↔ funnel post-cart.

default_position.description: Stage par défaut où cette offer apparaît dans le funnel. Override possible au niveau funnel.stages[].

expected_take_rate.description: Taux de prise estimé/observé pour calibrage funnel economics.

offer_groups.description: v2.0: offers are ALWAYS wrapped in a group, even a standalone offer (group of 1). Enables shared attributes with inheritance. Group-level fields cascade to all offers in the group unless overridden. Merge rule: objects deep-merge, arrays replace.

shared.description: Attributes inherited by all offers in this group. MERGE RULES: (1) nested objects merge deep — offer.pricing.price overrides group only on 'price' and keeps group 'currency'; (2) arrays replace entirely — offer.payment_options.methods replaces group.shared.payment_options.methods with no union; (3) scalars and booleans override explicitly — offer.subscription.trial.enabled:false overrides a shared true, treating the override as a conscious act. VALIDATION TIMING: the validator MUST resolve merge (shared→offer) BEFORE running JSON Schema validation on each offer. A raw un-merged offer may legitimately omit required fields that live in group.shared — only the resolved offer is checked against the base offer schema. Implementation: see resources/scripts/validate-all.py resolve_offer() step (added post v2.0 bump).

pricing.description: Shared pricing frame (currency, model defaults). Individual offers override price/savings.

subscription.description: Shared subscription frame. Typical use: group all frequency variants (weekly / monthly / quarterly) with same skip/pause/cancellation/trial rules; only frequency differs per offer.

shipping.description: Shared shipping config for all offers in group.

warranty.description: Shared warranty (typical hardware brands: Tushy, Frank Body).

incentives.description: Shared incentives that apply to all offers in group (e.g. a gift given regardless of which duration the customer picks, or a referral program that covers the whole group).

urgency.description: Shared urgency (flash sale window applying to whole group).

tags.description: Shared tags inherited by all offers in group.

type.description: v2.0: 10 structural types. Note: hardware bundles (Bruvi, Gozney) = type 'bundle' + tags[hardware] + requires_offer_id on the refill offer. Personalized offers (Care/of, Prose) = type 'subscription' (or 'bundle') + personalization{} pointer. Type describes SHAPE of data, not commercial strategy — use tags[] for the latter.

requires_offer_id.description: v2.0: prerequisite offer that must be owned before this one can be purchased. Typical case: consumables/refills that require the hardware bundle first. Ex: 'OFR-01' (coffee pods offer requires OFR-05 Bruvi machine). Null if standalone.

product_refs.description: v2.0: REQUIRED on every offer. Each offer declares its own product_refs explicitly — no inheritance of product_refs from group.shared (avoids ghost-offer bugs). Shared attributes live in offer_groups.shared, but product identity is always explicit per offer.

pricing.description: Offer-level pricing. Merges with group.shared.pricing (deep merge).

contents.description: Same structure as v1.8 contents (is_prepay, prepay_type, quantity, unit_name, duration, duration_unit, duration_type, duration_servings, cure_metadata, included_items, delivery_type, fulfillment_notes, fulfillment_delay, variant_selection). See v1.8 schema for full sub-properties.

subscription.description: Offer-level subscription config. Merges with group.shared.subscription. Typical pattern: group declares shipping/skip/pause/cancellation/trial once, offers declare per-frequency price + intro_phases.

intro_phases.description: v2.0 C3: cascading intro pricing replaces scalar intro_discount. Ordered phases, each applies from cycle N until the next phase starts. Ex: [{from_cycle:1, to_cycle:1, price:5}, {from_cycle:2, to_cycle:2, price:15}, {from_cycle:3, to_cycle:null, price:25}]. Null to_cycle = phase runs until churn. If empty, offer uses recurring_discount only.

commitment.description: v2.0 C6: structured commitment contract. Distinct from commitment_duration scalar (v1.8). Captures non_refundable, early_termination_fee, minimum_cycles.

membership.description: v2.0 C2 (type='membership' only): access-based offer with perks + catalog discount. Distinct from subscription (which ships one specific product on cadence). Typical: Amazon Prime, Thrive Market, Costco, Bruvi loyalty club. Null if type != 'membership'.

perks.description: Human-readable perks list ('free shipping', 'early access to drops', '10% off everything').

catalog_discount.description: Automatic discount applied to all (or filtered) offers in the brand catalog while membership is active.

includes_products.description: Products bundled with membership regardless of catalog purchase (Costco sample, welcome kit).

physical_shipment.description: true = membership also ships a physical item (welcome kit, member card). false = pure access.

product_refs.description: v2.0 C8: when type='free_product', structured product reference instead of freetext. Enables validators and downstream instances to resolve the actual product.

early_access.description: v2.0 C4 extension: cohort_tiers[] array replaces scalar cohort, lead_minutes supports sub-day drops.

cohort_tiers.description: Ordered tiers, each with its own lead time. Ex: [{cohort:'vip', lead_minutes:1440}, {cohort:'subscribers', lead_minutes:720}, {cohort:'email_list', lead_minutes:60}]. Tier 0 = earliest access.

personalization.description: v2.0 C5: pointer to external personalization logic (quiz, AI selector, profile-based filtering). offers.json only records WHICH mechanism applies — the rules engine lives in profile.json or a future config.json. Null if not personalized.

dynamic_pricing.description: true = final price depends on personalization output (Care/of supplement stack).

dynamic_contents.description: true = final product_refs depend on personalization output.
